raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "self-affirmation of epistemic discernment that distinguishes internal phenomenology from external correspondence",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "feelings constitute veridical data about the subject's internal condition",
    "feelings possess no guaranteed isomorphism with external states of affairs",
    "the subject possesses the capacity to recognize and maintain this distinction"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "coherent: cleanly separates first-person ontological status from third-person representational accuracy without internal contradiction",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the subject tends to over-literalize affective states as world-descriptions and benefits from a regulatory heuristic that preserves emotional data while suspending ontological commitment",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "emotional signals sometimes track objective features reliably, or the phrase 'information about my state' is interpreted so narrowly that any world-tracking function of affect is a priori excluded"
}
